title: Separating the Protein of Interest from MBP after TEV Protease Cleavage
author: New England Biolabs
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bfebjjan

steps.title: Gently shake the Ni Resin bottle...
steps.instruction: Gently shake the Ni Resin bottle to completely resuspend the slurry and transfer desired amount to a closed column; allow resin to settle.

steps.title: Open column and allow storage buffer to flow through
steps.instruction: Open column and allow storage buffer to flow through.

steps.title: Equilibrate the resin by adding 5...
steps.instruction: Equilibrate the resin by adding 5 column volumes (CV) of Binding buffer.

steps.title: Load the TEV Protease digestion directly...
steps.instruction: Load the TEV Protease digestion directly onto the equilibrated column and allow it to flow through the resin slowly (flow rate < 1 ml/minute). This provides sufficient time for the MBP-tag, TEV Protease, and any remaining MBP-fusion protein to effectively bind to the Ni resin.

steps.title: Collect the flow through containing the...
steps.instruction: Collect the flow through containing the protein of interest in a sterile tube.

steps.title: Wash the column with 1 CV...
steps.instruction: Wash the column with 1 CV of Binding Buffer to maximize the yield of the target protein; collect the flow through.

steps.title: Dialyze or buffer exchange target protein into desired storage buffer
steps.instruction: Dialyze or buffer exchange target protein into desired storage buffer.

steps.title: Add 1 Molarity (M), NaOH to...
steps.instruction: Add 1 Molarity (M), NaOH to the column and allow a contact time of 01:00:00 to 02:00:00 .

steps.title: Add 1
steps.instruction: Add 1.5 Molarity (M), NaCl to the column and allow a contact time of 00:10:00 – 00:15:00 followed by 10 CV of water.

steps.title: For long-term storage, NEBExpress Ni Resin...
steps.instruction: For long-term storage, NEBExpress Ni Resin should be stored in 20% ethanol at 2 – 8°C.
